notes: Ts/Tv ratio: expected ~2.1 for WGS SNPs. Freebayes (1.99) is closer to expectation than bcftools (1.26), suggesting bcftools may retain more false-positive transversions under these filter settings.
Freebayes calls MNPs (multi-nucleotide polymorphisms) which bcftools does not; this accounts for much of the difference in total count.
bcftools was run on a single sample without a panel of normals; strict filtering may explain the lower call count.
Runtime comparison: freebayes ran 3.8× faster (parallel per-chrom vs bcftools streaming mpileup).
